entries.definition:
sh–f–w to be near, to appear from a distance, to approach; to recover, to restore (to good health); to be on the brink, the extreme edge, the brink, the rim. Of this root, only شَفَا shafā occurs twice in the Qurʾan.
شَفَا shafā [n., in construct] brink of, edge of, rim of (3:103) وَكُنْتُمْ عَلَى شَفَا حُفْرَةٍ مِنَ النَّارِ فَأَنْقَذَكُمْ مِنْهَا and you were on the brink of a pit of Fire and He saved you from it.

sh–f–w to be near, to appear from a distance, to approach; to recover, to restore (to good health); to be on the brink, the extreme edge, the brink, the rim. Of this root, only شَفَا shafā occurs twice in the Qurʾan.
شَفَا shafā [n., in construct] brink of, edge of, rim of (3:103) وَكُنْتُمْ عَلَى شَفَا حُفْرَةٍ مِنَ النَّارِ فَأَنْقَذَكُمْ مِنْهَا and you were on the brink of a pit of Fire and He saved you from it.
